title: Repository State, References, and Revision Algebra · Guided Repository Studio
summary: A pairing session predicts each repository-state transition before building the first repository-state and revision-query proof for repository state, references, and revision algebra. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of repository-state and revision-query proof while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish direct refs, symbolic refs, pseudorefs, remote-tracking refs, packed refs, and detached HEAD using plumbing evidence.
Resolve revision names and disambiguate refs, object IDs, paths, reflog selectors, and peel operators before an operation.
Construct inclusive, exclusive, symmetric-difference, ancestry, parent, and path-limited revision sets for an exact question.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
workshop case — Case G92 is a workshop in a disposable repository. the audit mismatch is confined to commits reachable from exactly one reviewed tip. The learner must predict left and right markers expose exclusive ancestry without moving a reference, run git rev-list --left-right --ancestry-path release...topic -- src/policy.ts, explain two right-only policy commits and one left-only release fix define the review set, test this changed condition: replace topic with topic^{tree} and reject the query because a tree has no commit ancestry, and preserve this recovery boundary: read-only query; retain refs/audit/before-query at the reviewed release tip.

What must be established before implementation?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for distinguish direct refs, symbolic refs, pseudorefs, remote-tracking refs, packed refs, and detached head using plumbing evidence.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact
steps.instruction: Append an Advanced Git evidence block headed "# Evidence: gita-readonly-forensic-baseline-rjapa1" with exact before and after refs; a falsifiable prediction; the bounded simulator command; expected, observed, tree, behavior, changed-case, and recovery evidence; and one honest real-repository transfer limit.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Gita Readonly Forensic Baseline into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: gita-readonly-forensic-baseline-rjapa1
# scenario-key: trace-rjapa1 probe-1apajr boundary-apa1rj owner-rjapa1x fault-rjapa1z
# before-ref: main=a11cerj candidate=b00b1a1
# hypothesis: the audit mismatch is confined to commits reachable from exactly one reviewed tip
git rev-list --left-right --ancestry-path release...topic -- src/policy.ts
# expected: left and right markers expose exclusive ancestry without moving a reference
# observation: two right-only policy commits and one left-only release fix define the review set
# changed-case: replace topic with topic^{tree} and reject the query because a tree has no commit ancestry
# after-ref: main=a11cerj candidate=c0ffee1
# tree-proof: the affected tree and path set match the predicted repository transition
# behavior-proof: a changed input preserves the named product or release invariant
# recovery: read-only query; retain refs/audit/before-query at the reviewed release tip
# transfer-limit: verify revision parsing against installed Git help before applying the query to a real repository
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.
